Illyrio's first wife (unnamed, cousin of Prince of Pentos)

The girl sent to warm Tyrion's bed (unnamed)
Faith of the Seven (The Seven-Pointed Star quoted; the Father, the Mother, the Crone, the Warrior, the Smith, the Maid all named; High Septon referenced)
Old Gods (implied via standing stones raised by giants)
Andals (ancestral homeland identified; Hugor of the Hill)
Dothraki (horselords; khalasars; raids on the Flatlands)
Rhoynar (ironworking; Ghoyan Drohe; dwelt along the river)
Valyrians (roads; dragons; the Doom; the Freehold)
Lysene (Serra found in Lysene pillow house)
Hairy men (displaced by Andals; cousins to hairy men of Ib)
Free Folk of Ib (hairy men of Ib)
Illyrio's litter (purple velvet, silk pillows, goose down)
Valyrian sphinx (female — dragon body, woman's face)
Valyrian sphinx (male — missing, taken to Vaes Dothrak)
Statue by Illyrio's pool (carved by Pytho Malanon)
Golden chain / golden hands (used to strangle Shae)
The Seven-Pointed Star (holy book of the Faith; two passages quoted)

mentions.raw_name: Dragons (three living dragons hatched by Daenerys; historical dragons of Valyria that destroyed Ghoyan Drohe)

Stone men (on the Rhoyne; "poor damned things")
The Doom of Valyria (referenced — Valyrian roads endure four centuries after)
War of the Ninepenny Kings (Selmy slew last Blackfyre Pretender)
Battle of the Blackwater (Tyrion references losing his nose)
Battle of Qohor (sellswords couldn't stand against Dothraki)
